title: Mean-centering commutes with scalar multiplication for finite signals

source.citation: Lloyd N. Trefethen, Spectral Methods in MATLAB, SIAM, 2000, Ch. 3, pp. 17-28.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: Mean-centering a scalar multiple equals the scalar multiple of the mean-centered signal.

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteMean
declarations.statement: def finiteMean {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := finiteDC s u / (s.card : Real)

declarations.name: centeredSignal
declarations.statement: def centeredSignal {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (i : ι) : Real := u i - finiteMean s u

declarations.name: centeredSignal_smul
declarations.statement: theorem centeredSignal_smul {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (c : Real) (i : ι) : centeredSignal s (fun j => c * u j) i = c * centeredSignal s u i

tasks.input: Translate the finite-index spectral/DC identity `centeredSignal_smul` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem centeredSignal_smul {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (c : Real) (i : ι) : centeredSignal s (fun j => c * u j) i = c * centeredSignal s u i
Unfold centeredSignal, use finiteMean_smul, and ring-normalize.

tasks.input: Identify the main finite-sum or mean-centering lemma needed for the proof.
